summary.resolution: stage-by-stage browser runtime probe plus media-backed Galaga challenge labels and current path-vector comparison
summary.startingAssumption: Each challenge stage starts at 1.0/10 interesting factor and must earn credit through measured safety, path reference match, visual novelty, alien variety, and durable evidence.
summary.strongestFinding: Sampled challenge windows preserve the Galaga-like no-shot/no-ship-loss rule.
summary.weakestFinding: challenge stages are still not authored enough as memorable Galaga-like set pieces: 3 sampled stage(s) still best-match the same Galaga challenge-2 reference, stage 3 now lands on the first-challenge bee-line reference but needs stronger trajectory precision, active sprite-motion novelty is unscored, and stage 19 lacks reference grounding.
summary.playerMeaning: A player should experience challenging stages as safe but tense score exhibitions with memorable entry routes and alien novelty. Aurora currently has the safety rule, but the visual choreography is not yet distinctive enough.
summary.designerMeaning: Design work should move from broad path-family labels to explicit per-challenge contracts: group order, entry side, exit side, alien family, motion phases, bonus opportunity, and result feedback.

stageRows.runtimeFirstWaveTypes: bee, bee, bee, bee, but, but, but, but
stageRows.galagaTarget: First Galaga-style challenging stage: readable bonus set piece, no fire, no ship loss, upper-band mirrored entries, bee/butterfly line waves, visible arrival/peel-off.
stageRows.criticalExpectation: Should teach the player that challenge stages are safe, scoreable pattern reads rather than combat waves.
stageRows.galagaReferenceMeaning: upper-band mirrored group with no enemy fire
stageRows.currentRead: first-challenge-peel / first-challenge-peel; lanes bee, bee, bee, bee, but, but, but, but; first-wave types bee, bee, bee, bee, but, but, but, but; visual families classic.
stageRows.graphicsRead: Current graphics show classic family styling with bee, but alien types. This is still a proxy for visual identity; no active sprite-motion phase score is attached to the challenge window yet.
stageRows.movementRead: Trajectory vector best-match score 4.2/10 against challenge-1-arrival-group-1; xRange 0.4685, yRange 0.2039, pathLength 0.2711.
stageRows.alienVariationRead: Opening wave exposes 2 type(s) and classic visual family labels; later-stage novelty is not yet backed by sprite-motion scoring.
nextActions: Protect the first-challenge bee/butterfly line contract, then tune path length, turn count, and rack-slot precision against challenge-1 arrival and late-wave labels.
Add contact-sheet comparison for first-visible frame, entry side, exit side, lane occupancy, and group timing so the next tuning pass can improve trajectory precision without subjective guessing.

stageRows.runtimeFirstWaveTypes: but, boss, rogue, bee, bee, rogue, boss, but
stageRows.galagaTarget: Second challenge should feel denser and more novel than challenge 1 while staying nonlethal and non-shooting.
stageRows.criticalExpectation: Should introduce a stronger mixed-family visual grammar and a learnable crossing pattern.
stageRows.galagaReferenceMeaning: denser second challenge pattern with visible upper-field score window

stageRows.alienVariationRead: Opening wave exposes 4 type(s) and classic visual family labels; later-stage novelty is not yet backed by sprite-motion scoring.
criticalGaps: Cross-sweep identity is visible in labels, but the measured vector still lands closest to the same challenge-2 reference as most other challenge stages.
nextActions: Tune challenge 2 toward the denser mixed-novelty-line reference instead of relying on a generic cross-sweep.
Score separate group identities so stage 7 is not just a slightly wider stage 3.

improvementPlan: Separate rule conformance from interest conformance in challenge metrics.
Stage 3: implement reference-labeled first-challenge entry and peel-off groups.
Stage 7: deepen challenge-2 mixed-novelty pattern and group scoring.
Stage 11: add active sprite-motion scoring around dragonfly/boss-led novelty.
Stage 15/19: acquire or label late challenge reference targets before late-stage tuning.
Publish contact sheets, trajectory SVGs, and per-stage critical gaps in generated docs.
